steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The decisive clue is the phrase “the reductions inspired an air-cooling device” in the final sentence, which directly shows how the underlined observation functions as the basis for a practical application.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content: The underlined observation (air cooling around mushrooms) is the key finding central to the text's discussion of how the research produced a practical application. Choice A correctly names this function. Choices B, C, and D misidentify the function—B treats it as evidence for an earlier claim when it actually enables a later application, while C and D reverse the causal order. That matches choice A — A is correct.
